name: Swiss Bircher Muesli

ingredients.ingredient: Oats (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)
Apples, raw, gala, with skin (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps: Soak the oats in the milk overnight in the fridge.
Stir in the grated apple just before serving.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Oats (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)
Apples, raw, gala, with skin (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps_detailed: Combine the oats and milk in a bowl or jar, cover, and refrigerate overnight so the oats soften fully.
The next morning, grate the apple and toss it with the lime juice to keep it from browning.
Stir the grated apple and honey into the soaked oats just before serving.
Created by a Swiss physician in the early 1900s as a healthy breakfast for his patients, Bircher muesli remains a Swiss breakfast staple worldwide.

name: Swiss Roesti with Egg
steps: Squeeze excess liquid from the grated potato.
Press into a thin, even layer in the buttered pan and fry 6-8 minutes per side until deeply golden and crisp.
Top with the fried egg.
steps_detailed: Grate the potato and squeeze out as much excess liquid as possible using a clean towel.
Season with black pepper and press into a thin, even layer in the buttered pan.
Fry over medium heat, 7-9 minutes per side, pressing down occasionally, until deeply golden and crisp on both sides.
Top with a fried egg - roesti began as a hearty farmer's breakfast in the canton of Bern and became a Swiss national symbol.

name: Swiss Zopf with Jam
steps: Mix the flour, butter, and milk into a smooth, slightly enriched dough.
Shape into a simple braid or loaf and bake at 375F for 20-25 minutes until golden.
Serve sliced with the preserves.
steps_detailed: Mix the flour, butter, milk, egg, and sugar into a smooth, elastic dough. Knead briefly and rest 20-30 minutes.
Divide into strands and braid into a simple loaf, or shape into a round loaf if simpler.
Bake at 375F (190C) for 22-28 minutes until deeply golden.
Serve sliced, generously spread with the preserves - zopf is the classic Swiss Sunday morning bread.

name: Swiss Cheese Fondue
steps: Toss the grated cheeses with the flour.
Warm the wine gently, then add the cheese in handfuls, stirring until melted and smooth.
Serve warm with bread cubes for dipping.
steps_detailed: Rub the inside of a small pot with the minced garlic.
Toss the grated cheeses with the flour to help them melt smoothly.
Warm the wine in the pot over low heat, then add the cheese gradually in handfuls, stirring constantly in a figure-eight motion until fully melted and smooth.
Season with a pinch of nutmeg.
Serve warm with cubes of crusty bread for dipping - Switzerland's most iconic communal dish.

name: Swiss Roesti with Ham
steps: Squeeze excess liquid from the grated potato and mix with the diced ham.
Press into the buttered pan and fry 6-8 minutes per side until crisp and golden.
Slice into wedges and serve.
steps_detailed: Grate the potato and squeeze out as much liquid as possible, then mix with the diced ham, onion, and black pepper.
Press into a thin, even layer in the buttered pan.
Fry over medium heat, 7-9 minutes per side, pressing down occasionally, until deeply golden and crisp.
Slice into wedges and serve - a heartier version of the classic roesti, popular as a full lunch.

name: Swiss Barley Soup (Buendner Gerstensuppe)
steps: Saute the bacon and carrot until fragrant.
Add the barley and broth, and simmer 35-40 minutes until the barley is tender.
Season and serve hot.
steps_detailed: Saute the diced bacon, onion, carrot, and leek together for 6-8 minutes until softened and fragrant.
Add the barley and beef broth, and bring to a simmer.
Cover and cook over low heat 40-45 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the barley is tender and the soup has thickened.
A hearty specialty from the canton of Graubuenden, traditionally simmered for hours with dried meats.

name: Swiss Alplermagronen
steps: Boil the noodles and potato together until both are tender, about 12-15 minutes.
Drain and stir in the milk and cheese until melted and creamy.
Serve with applesauce on the side, the traditional pairing.
steps_detailed: Boil the noodles and potato together in salted water until both are tender, 12-16 minutes, then drain.
Saute the onion in the butter until deeply golden and caramelized, 10-12 minutes.
Return the drained noodles and potato to the pot, stir in the milk and grated cheese over low heat until melted and creamy.
Top with the caramelized onion and serve with applesauce alongside - a beloved alpine dish, named for the cowherds (Alpler) who traditionally made it high in the mountains.

name: Swiss Raclette
steps: Boil the potato until tender.
Melt the cheese under the broiler until bubbling and lightly browned.
Pour the melted cheese over the potato and serve with the onion.
steps_detailed: Boil the potato whole until fully tender, 15-20 minutes, then keep warm.
Arrange the sliced cheese in a small oven-safe dish and broil for 3-5 minutes until fully melted, bubbling, and lightly browned in spots.
Pour the melted cheese directly over the warm boiled potato, season with black pepper, and serve with the pickled or sliced onion - raclette is as much a social gathering as a meal in Switzerland.

name: Swiss Zurcher Geschnetzeltes
steps: Saute the chicken and mushrooms until the chicken is cooked through.
Add the wine and simmer 3-4 minutes, then stir in the sour cream.
Serve over roesti or noodles.
steps_detailed: Saute the onion in the butter until soft, then add the chicken and mushrooms, cooking over medium-high heat until the chicken is just cooked through and lightly browned, 6-8 minutes.
Dust with the flour and stir to coat, then add the wine, scraping up any browned bits, and simmer 3-4 minutes.
Stir in the sour cream and simmer 2-3 more minutes until the sauce is smooth and slightly thickened.
Serve over roesti or buttered noodles - a beloved dish from Zurich, sliced veal traditionally used in place of chicken.

name: Swiss Cordon Bleu
steps: Pound the chicken thin, then layer with the ham and cheese, and fold or roll to enclose.
Dredge in flour and pan-fry until golden and cooked through, about 5-6 minutes per side.
Serve with roesti or a simple salad.
steps_detailed: Pound the chicken breast to an even thickness, then layer the ham and cheese in the center and fold the chicken over to fully enclose the filling, securing with a toothpick if needed.
Dredge in flour, then egg, pressing to help it adhere.
Heat the oil in a skillet over medium heat and fry 5-7 minutes per side until deeply golden and cooked through, with the cheese melted inside.
Serve with roesti or a simple green salad - a Swiss dish now popular worldwide.

name: Swiss Papet Vaudois
steps: Simmer the sausage with the leeks and potato in water to cover for 25-30 minutes until tender.
Mash some of the potato into the broth with the milk to thicken slightly.
Slice the sausage and serve with the leek and potato mixture.
steps_detailed: Simmer the sausage with the leeks and potato in water to just cover for 30-35 minutes until the potato is very tender.
Remove the sausage and slice it.
Mash some of the potato directly in the pot with the milk, butter, and black pepper to thicken the leek mixture into a soft, stew-like consistency.
Serve the sliced sausage over the leek and potato mixture - a classic dish from the canton of Vaud, historically paired with saucisson au chou.

name: Swiss Cheese and Bread
steps: Slice the cheese and serve with the bread and a small spoonful of preserves.
steps_detailed: Slice the cheese and arrange on a small plate with the bread.
Serve with a small spoonful of cranberry sauce and a scattering of chopped walnuts - a simple, classic Swiss cheese board pairing.

name: Swiss Rosti Bites
steps: Squeeze excess liquid from the grated potato and mix with the grated cheese.
Shape into small patties and fry in the butter, 3-4 minutes per side, until golden.
Serve warm.
steps_detailed: Grate the potato and squeeze out excess liquid, then mix with the grated cheese and black pepper.
Shape into small patties, about 2 inches across.
Heat the butter in a skillet over medium heat and fry the patties 3-5 minutes per side until golden and the cheese has melted inside.
Serve warm as a simple, cheesy snack.

name: Swiss Meringues with Cream

ingredients.note: tbsp, whipped, as a cream stand-in

steps: Whip the egg white with the sugar until stiff and glossy.
Pipe or spoon small mounds onto a lined sheet and bake at 200F for 1.5-2 hours until dry and crisp.
Serve with the whipped cream.

ingredients_full.note: tbsp, whipped with a little sugar

steps_detailed: Whip the egg whites until foamy, then gradually add the sugar, whipping until very stiff and glossy.
Fold in the vanilla.
Pipe or spoon small mounds onto a parchment-lined sheet.
Bake at 200F (95C) for 1.5-2 hours until completely dry and crisp throughout, then let cool in the turned-off oven.
Serve with the whipped cream - meringues (meringue) are widely believed to have originated in the Swiss town of Meiringen.

name: Swiss Landjager-Style Snack Sausage
steps: Slice the sausage and serve with the cheese, no cooking needed.
steps_detailed: Slice the sausage into thin rounds (Landjager is traditionally a dried, cured snack sausage, eaten cold and often carried on hikes).
Serve alongside the sliced cheese, with a pinch of caraway seed scattered over if you'd like, echoing the spicing found in some regional versions.
A simple, portable Swiss snack, popular for hiking in the Alps.

name: Swiss Chocolate Fondue

ingredients.ingredient: Ghirardelli 100% Unsweetened Premium Baking Cocoa Powder, Unsweetened

steps: Warm the milk with the sugar and cocoa powder, whisking until smooth and glossy.
Serve warm with the strawberries for dipping.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Ghirardelli 100% Unsweetened Premium Baking Cocoa Powder, Unsweetened
Apples, raw, gala, with skin (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps_detailed: Warm the milk in a small pot, then whisk in the cocoa powder, sugar, and butter until completely smooth and glossy.
Simmer gently 2-3 minutes, stirring, until slightly thickened.
Transfer to a small warm bowl and serve with the strawberries and apple slices for dipping - Switzerland, the birthplace of much of the world's finest chocolate, does this beautifully.

name: Swiss Nusstorte
steps: Make a simple pastry from the flour and butter, and press into a small tart tin.
Simmer the brown sugar with a splash of water and the walnuts into a thick, sticky filling.
Fill the crust and bake at 350F for 25-30 minutes until set.
steps_detailed: Make a simple pastry from the flour, half the butter, a pinch of salt, and cold water. Press into a small tart tin.
Melt the remaining butter with the brown sugar, milk, and honey, and simmer 3-4 minutes until it thickens slightly.
Stir in the chopped walnuts and pour into the crust.
Bake at 350F (175C) for 28-35 minutes until the filling is set and bubbling gently at the edges.
Cool before slicing - a specialty of the Engadin valley, this dense, caramel-walnut tart keeps well for days.

name: Swiss Basler Lackerli
steps: Warm the honey and mix with the flour, walnuts, and cinnamon into a firm dough.
Press into a small pan and bake at 350F for 15-18 minutes until set.
Cool and cut into small bars.
steps_detailed: Warm the honey gently until runny, then mix with the flour, baking powder, cinnamon, and allspice.
Stir in the chopped walnuts, forming a firm, sticky dough.
Press evenly into a small buttered pan.
Bake at 350F (175C) for 15-20 minutes until set and lightly golden at the edges.
Let cool completely, then cut into small bars - a spiced honey-nut treat from Basel, traditionally associated with Christmas.

name: Swiss Fondue Dipping Sauce
steps: Whisk the mustard, honey, and sour cream together.
Serve alongside roesti or grilled meats.
steps_detailed: Whisk the mustard, honey, sour cream, and black pepper together until smooth.
Serve alongside roesti, grilled meats, or as a dip for cheese fondue leftovers.

name: Swiss Herb Butter
steps: Mash the softened butter with the dill and garlic until well combined.
Use on bread, roesti, or grilled meats.
steps_detailed: Mash the softened butter with the dill, garlic, and black pepper until well combined and evenly flecked with green.
Roll into a log in plastic wrap and chill until firm, or use immediately.
Spread on bread, melt over roesti, or top grilled meats and fish.
